videos.title: The Hard Work Paradox In Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
The Hard Work Paradox In Hockey The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: The Real Reason You're Bloated After Eating
videos.summary_source_text:
The Real Reason You're Bloated After Eating Why am I bloated after eating? Bloating after eating is often linked to poor digestive health, not just the food you ate. Learn how to relieve stomach bloating and improve digestion naturally by addressing the root cause.

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/3Rfk5FP

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

Fix your gut with one microbe: ▶️ https://youtu.be/nZV1oYv5Ddo

How to make L. Reuteri yogurt: ➡️ https://drbrg.co/4wokndO
[Affiliate links]
Yogurt maker: https://amzn.to/4rYCu6w
Yogurt jars: https://amzn.to/411hSPw 
Yogurt containers: https://amzn.to/411eXX0

0:00 Introduction: Why you feel bloated after eating
1:19 Digestive health and belly bloating 
3:45 The gallbladder and the pancreas
5:27 SIBO and the gut microbiome 
8:58 Stomach bloating location explained
10:52 Low stomach acid and belly bloating 
16:26 Bloating remedies

Keep watching to discover the best ways to improve gut health and eliminate bloating naturally:
▶️ https://youtu.be/jZ4jNALF9Ys
▶️ https://youtu.be/RCjQAQuWiYc
▶️ https://youtu.be/zXd86RvNzZA

Low stomach acid is a common problem and may be the reason you feel bloated after eating. Healthy stomach acid is essential for proper digestion and helps trigger the release of bile, which is critical for breaking down fats and absorbing nutrients.

Bile is produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. When bile flow or gallbladder function is impaired, digestion can suffer and place additional stress on the pancreas.

The pancreas produces insulin as well as digestive enzymes needed to break down food properly. Poor digestion can contribute to stomach bloating, discomfort, and nutrient absorption issues.

Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) is another common cause of bloating after meals. Poor gut health and sluggish digestion can also lead to constipation and ongoing digestive problems.

Even the specific location of your stomach bloating may provide clues about the root cause of your digestive issues.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

Dr. Eric Berg received his Doctor of Chiropractic degree from Palmer College of Chiropractic in 1988. His use of “doctor” or “Dr.” in relation to himself solely refers to that degree. 0:00 Introduction: Why you feel bloated after eating
1:19 Digestive health and belly bloating 
3:45 The gallbladder and the pancreas
5:27 SIBO and the gut microbiome

Keep watching to discover the best ways to improve gut health and eliminate bloating naturally:

▶️ https://youtu.be/zXd86RvNzZA

Low stomach acid is a common problem and may be the reason you feel bloated after eating. Healthy stomach acid is essential for proper digestion and helps trigger the release of bile, which is critical for breaking down fats and absorbing nutrients.

Bile is produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. When bile flow or gallbladder function is impaired, digestion can suffer and place additional stress on the pancreas.

The pancreas produces insulin as well as digestive enzymes needed to break down food properly. Poor digestion can contribute to stomach bloating, discomfort, and nutrient absorption issues.

Small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO) is another common cause of bloating after meals. Poor gut health and sluggish digestion can also lead to constipation and ongoing digestive problems.

Even the specific location of your stomach bloating may provide clues about the root cause of your digestive issues.

videos.title: This Vitamin Deficiency May Be Triggering Your Dizziness
videos.summary_source_text:
This Vitamin Deficiency May Be Triggering Your Dizziness Can you guess the #1 vitamin deficiency behind vertigo? 

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

DATA:
https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00405-019-05694-0
https://vitamindwiki.com/tiki-index.php?page_id=3460#BPPV_eliminated_by_Vitamin_D_April_2019
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23096068/

0:00 Introduction: BPPV explained 
0:38 What causes vertigo? 
1:15 The Dix-Hallpike test
3:00 The Epley maneuver
4:48 What causes calcium crystals? 
7:35 The best remedy for BPPV
9:00 Learn more about vitamin D deficiency! 

Today I want to cover a very specific type of vertigo called benign paroxysmal positional vertigo (BPPV). 

This type of vertigo makes you feel like you’re spinning when you’re not, and it’s the #1 cause of dizziness. 

This condition is typically caused by a calcium crystal that has broken off and is in the canals of the inner ear that control equilibrium and balance. 

There is a simple test called the Dix-Hallpike test that you can do at home to help determine if you have this type of vertigo. 

Check with your doctor before doing this test to ensure you don’t have an issue with your cervical spine that could prevent you from rotating your neck. 

After you perform the test and determine which side is causing a problem, you can try the Epley maneuver. The goal of this maneuver is to create different motions to slowly move the calcium crystal out of the ear.

An interesting systematic review and meta-analysis done in 2020 showed an association between low vitamin D and BPPV. 

Vitamin D supports the absorption and transportation of calcium. It's important to note that some people have a genetic issue that causes normal vitamin D levels in the blood but not the tissues. 

I believe one of the best natural remedies for BPPV is a combination of vitamin D3 and vitamin K2. For every 10,000 IU of vitamin D3, you would want 100 mcg of vitamin K2. Talk to your doctor about taking 50,000 IU of vitamin D3 and 500 mcg of vitamin K2 once a week for BPPV.

When using high doses of vitamin D, always check with your doctor. High doses of vitamin D can increase blood/urine calcium levels resulting in calcium deposits in the kidney. Also drink 2.5 liters of fluids per day, take cofactors with vitamin D (magnesium, K2 and zinc) and have your doctor monitor your PTH, vitamin D blood levels and creatinine and avoid excess calcium supplements of high calcium foods when taking vitamin D levels over 10,000 IUs.

videos.title: 9.5: Wallstedt dazzles with 35-save night, ties Roloson in Wild history books 🇸🇪⭐
videos.summary_source_text:
9.5: Wallstedt dazzles with 35-save night, ties Roloson in Wild history books 🇸🇪⭐ Don't call it a comeback! Jesper Wallstedt is simply sensational in Game 3, stopping 35 of 36 shots the Avalanche send his way to help lead his Wild to a huge 5-1 victory - their first of the series. The win is Wallstedt's 5th of these playoffs, tying him with Dwayne Roloson (5 wins in 2003) for the most in a single postseason in Wild history! Wallstedt's incredible night in the crease earns him both 3rd star of the game and Top European Player of the Night honors!

videos.title: Minnesota Controls the Action, Gets Back Into Series With a Game 3 Win Over the Avalanche
videos.summary_source_text:
Minnesota Controls the Action, Gets Back Into Series With a Game 3 Win Over the Avalanche Minnesota Wild overpower Colorado Avalanche with relentless play and tactical precision. After dropping their first game in seven playoff outings, the Avalanche face pressing questions about their offensive cohesion and ability to adjust. Zach Pearson and Chris Micieli break down how Kirill Kaprizov’s opportunistic goal, Ryan Hartman’s power play presence, and Brock Faber’s net-front drive set the tone for the Wild’s 5-1 victory. Was Colorado’s signature double drop pass power play strategy finally exposed, and can Jared Bednar’s squad recover?

The hosts examine Nathan MacKinnon’s frustration, Scott Wedgewood’s short night in goal, and the Avalanche’s ongoing battle with zone entry and board battles. They debate whether Mackenzie Blackwood should start next, highlight Minnesota’s defensive lockdown, and discuss controversial calls involving Quinn Hughes. How will Colorado respond after a rare playoff letdown? Don’t miss this spirited, stat-packed analysis of the Avalanche’s quest to regain control of their Stanley Cup run.

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 3 | Truist Championship | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | Round 3 | Truist Championship | 2026 Check out the best shots of the day from Round 3 of the 2026 Truist Championship, featuring Alex Fitzpatrick, Kristoffer Reitan, Cameron Young, and Sungjae Im, among others. 

The PGA TOUR heads to Quail Hollow Club in Charlotte, North Carolina, for the Truist Championship, one of the standout events on the calendar. Rory McIlroy returns to a venue where he has enjoyed incredible success and headlines a star-studded field. He is joined by a host of top talent including Cameron Young, Xander Schauffele, Justin Thomas, Tommy Fleetwood, Matt Fitzpatrick and Justin Rose, as players continue their push for FedExCup points during a crucial stretch of the season. Known for its demanding closing stretch, the “Green Mile,” Quail Hollow promises drama and excitement down the back nine. With elite talent, iconic holes, and high stakes, expect plenty of standout performances throughout the week.

videos.title: Anton Lundell Talks Missing Playoffs for First Time with Florida Panthers
videos.summary_source_text:
Anton Lundell Talks Missing Playoffs for First Time with Florida Panthers Florida Panthers center Anton Lundell meets the media after his team wrapped up its season at the IcePlex in Fort Lauderdale on April 16, 2026. 

This is the first time in Lundell’s five seasons the Panthers missed the playoffs.

Lundell has joined captain Sasha Barkov on Team Finland for the IIHF World Championship.

The Panthers won three straight to end the season but missed the Stanley Cup Playoffs. 

Florida won the past two Stanley Cup championships. 

The two-time Stanley Cup champions will miss the playoffs for the first time since 2019. 

Lundell was a first-round pick by Florida in 2020 and made his debut with the team in 2021.

Video Courtesy Florida Panthers / @FlaPanthers / Panthers Vision

The Panthers Have Done It Again, Repeat as Cup Champs: https://floridahockeynow.com/florida-panthers-repeat-stanley-cup-champions-2025/

videos.title: Your mental swing path is not real…(Righthanded)
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
Your mental swing path is not real…(Righthanded) The rotation or turn of our body will make you feel one thing but execute another. Almost giving up I started to maximize the out feeling and found the solution. #golftips #pga #golfswing 

0:00 Intro to the mental and real swing path

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 The rotation or turn of our body will make you feel one thing but execute another. Almost giving up I started to maximize the out feeling and found the solution. #golftips #pga #golfswing

videos.title: Your mental swing path is not real…(Lefthanded)
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
Your mental swing path is not real…(Lefthanded) The rotation or turn of our body will make you feel one thing but execute another. Almost giving up I started to maximize the out feeling and found the solution. #golftips #pga #golfswing 

0:00 Intro to the mental and real swing path

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 The rotation or turn of our body will make you feel one thing but execute another. Almost giving up I started to maximize the out feeling and found the solution. #golftips #pga #golfswing

videos.title: Just 1/8 Teaspoon a Day Helps Protect Your Brain as You Age | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
Just 1/8 Teaspoon a Day Helps Protect Your Brain as You Age | Dr. Mandell What if one of the most powerful things for your brain was already sitting in your kitchen right now? In this video, we explore the fascinating science behind cinnamon and how just one eighth teaspoon a day may help support brain health, memory, circulation, blood sugar balance, and healthy aging. Researchers are studying how certain compounds inside cinnamon may influence inflammation, oxidative stress, insulin sensitivity, glucose metabolism, and even proteins linked to neurodegeneration and Alzheimer’s disease. You’ll learn why blood sugar spikes can damage delicate brain tissue over time, how insulin resistance affects memory and cognition, and why stabilizing glucose may be one of the most important things you can do for long term brain protection. We’ll also discuss the difference between Ceylon cinnamon and cassia cinnamon, the safest way to use it daily, and why tiny consistent habits can create powerful long term health benefits. Watch until the end and discover why this simple kitchen spice may become one of the easiest daily habits to support your brain as you age.

Tour Pros Try to Hit the Perfect 150-Yard Shot Filmed ahead of the Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship, and marking Estrella Damm’s 150-year anniversary, Pablo Larrazábal and Rafa Cabrera Bello go head-to-head as they’re challenged to hit every club in their bag 150 yards.

The rules are simple, whoever finishes closest to the 150-yard line with each shot wins a point. With 14 points up for grabs, let’s find out who comes out on top.

videos.title: Det mentala och det verkliga svingspåret…
videos.channel: Bättre & Enklare golf med Marcus Edblad PGA
videos.summary_source_text:
Det mentala och det verkliga svingspåret… Om vi vill ändra svingspår behöver vi förstå hur rotationen påverkar oss. För att svinga mer utåt behöver vi överdriva otroligt mycket för att få det vi vill. #golf #golfswing #golftips 


Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du fightas med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook. Om vi vill ändra svingspår behöver vi förstå hur rotationen påverkar oss. För att svinga mer utåt behöver vi överdriva otroligt mycket för att få det vi vill. #golf #golfswing #golftips

videos.title: I Was Coached Wrong for Years. So I Spent 10 Years Changing Hockey Coaching
videos.summary_source_text:
I Was Coached Wrong for Years. So I Spent 10 Years Changing Hockey Coaching I Was Coached Wrong for Years. So I Spent 10 Years Changing Hockey Coaching.

Train 2.0 didn't start as a business plan. It started as frustration. I kept asking 
my coaches why. Why this drill, why this technique, why this way  and the answers didn't hold up. So I went headfirst into the science of how elite players actually move and began my undergrade in Kinesiology at UBC. 
 
What I found was a complete disconnect between what the NHL's best were 
doing and what traditional coaching was teaching.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: GPT-6 Explained: Release Date, New Features & The Hidden Truth
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT-6 Explained: Release Date, New Features & The Hidden Truth Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everything we actually know about GPT-6 — release date, new features, the real timeline, and the risks nobody's talking about. I spent weeks digging through OpenAI announcements, Sam Altman interviews, and leaked roadmaps to separate hype from reality.

In this video, you'll learn:
✅ When GPT-6 is actually launching (late 2026 / early 2027)
✅ The biggest upgrades: long-term memory, real-time video, agent mode
✅ How GPT-6 compares to GPT-5, GPT-5.5 ("Spud"), and earlier models
✅ Why ChatGPT's growth is plateauing — and how GPT-6 fits OpenAI's strategy
✅ Real-world use cases: developers, creatives, healthcare, customer service
✅ The risks: misinformation, privacy, job displacement, malicious use

videos.title: Round 3 Highlights | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship
videos.summary_source_text:
Round 3 Highlights | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship Highlights from round three of the Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship at Real Club de Golf El Prat in Barcelona.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The DP World Tour is back to where it all started this week. Spain was the host of the first ever DP World Tour - then European Tour - event in 1972 so it is fitting that it is where the 2026 European Swing gets started. The fourth of five Global Swings on the 2026 Race to Dubai, the European Swing consists of six counting events - four of which are national opens - with the US PGA Championship and U.S. Open also happening within the same timeframe. Spain has long been one of the world's great golfing nations and this week local heroes will take centre stage, with a string of established and rising stars searching for home glory. Nacho Elvira has already won in 2026 and will be hungry for more, as will fellow winners in recent seasons Eugenio Chacarra, Alejandro del Rey, Angel Hidalgo and Pablo Larrazábal.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

videos.title: Dags för studenten men vet inte vad jag ska ge - vad tänker du?
videos.summary_source_text:
Dags för studenten men vet inte vad jag ska ge - vad tänker du? De flesta som tar studenten får pengar i kuvert, swish, blommor eller någon pryl de inte behöver. Vi tänker att det finns något bättre: ge 500 kronor, sätt dig 30 minuter med studenten, öppna ett ISK tillsammans, förklara ränta-på-ränta. Och en uppföljning ett år senare.

Det är inte beloppet som räknas — det är tiden. Ge med varm hand, även till en 19-åring i studentmössa.

Vad var den bästa studentpresenten du fick? Skriv i kommentarerna 👇

videos.title: Stop Shooting Like It's 1992. Here's How Modern Snipers Do It.
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Shooting Like It's 1992. Here's How Modern Snipers Do It. Iginla and Sakic ushered in the next evolution of shooting in hockey and decades later, most players are still being taught like it's 1987.

The science is clear. Modern sticks are engineered to flex, and the players who finish at a high level are the ones who let the stick do the work. Rotate the body, let the puck lag behind, whip the stick through, and the flex generates power you can't create through muscle alone. It's a mechanical advantage, not a strength contest.

But step into most rinks and you'll still see kids being coached to wind up and whack the puck like they're swinging an axe. 

We reverse-engineered what modern snipers like Bedard, Matthews and Pettersson do, built the Slingshot Shooting System around it, and the result is a shot that takes less effort and produces more velocity for any player willing to trade muscling through for moving correctly.

videos.title: Before Hitting Your Woods Just DO THIS For 5 Seconds
videos.summary_source_text:
Before Hitting Your Woods Just DO THIS For 5 Seconds Struggling to make consistent contact with your fairway woods and hybrids? You're not alone — and you're not doing anything drastically wrong. These are the longest clubs in your bag with the least amount of loft, so be easy on yourself. But there is something you can feel before every single shot that will transform how consistently you strike them.

Data from Shot Scope tells us that mid to high handicap golfers use fairway woods and hybrids 25% of the time. That's a quarter of every round. Improve your consistency with these clubs and the effect on your score is enormous. In this fairway wood lesson I want to show you exactly what I showed my students John and Tom this week — two simple things that control everything about how your hybrid and fairway wood strikes the ground, delivers loft and gets the ball flying high and long.

The first is about controlling the depth of your arc — how the club interacts with the ground rather than where it lands. Get this right and the topping, the fatting and the inconsistent contact start to disappear almost immediately. The second is the prop drill — something I've shared before on the channel but which Tom was getting slightly wrong, and I want to make sure you're doing it correctly because when it clicks it is genuinely a game changer for your fairway wood ball striking.

⬇️ FREE PRACTICE GUIDE — Everything from this fairway wood lesson in one free download right here: https://www.dannymaude.com/how-to-hit=fairway-woods-and-hybrids

What you'll learn in this fairway wood and hybrid lesson:
▶ Why fairway woods and hybrids are harder to hit — and why that's not your fault
▶ The three things you need to control for consistent fairway wood contact every time
▶ The shoulder feel that keeps your arc intact and stops you topping fairway woods
▶ The prop drill for fairway woods — and the common mistake that stops it working
▶ How to control where your club lands relative to the golf ball every time
▶ How to deliver loft consistently so your fairway woods fly high and go further
▶ Why this approach works for hybrids and irons too — not just fairway woods

⏱️ CHAPTERS
0:00 — Why fairway woods and hybrids are so hard to hit consistently
1:02 — The three things that control your fairway wood contact
1:22 — Thing 1: Controlling the depth of your arc
2:28 — John's story: the most common depth of arc problem
3:18 — The shoulder feel that fixes your arc immediately
4:42 — How to practise this before every fairway wood shot
5:40 — Thing 2: Controlling where the club lands
6:44 — The prop drill for fairway woods — done correctly
8:40 — Tom's story: what most golfers get wrong with the prop drill
9:23 — The prop drill in action — high draw with a hybrid
11:03 — Putting both together for consistent fairway wood ball striking

This fairway wood lesson works for all levels — whether you're a beginner trying to understand why your hybrids and fairway woods feel so inconsistent, a mid-handicapper who tops or fats these clubs far too often, or someone who hits them well occasionally but can never repeat it. Given that you're reaching for these clubs on a quarter of every hole you play, improving your fairway wood contact is one of the fastest ways to lower your scores without changing anything else in your game. Two simple feels. Consistent contact. More greens in two.

If this helped your fairway woods and hybrids, hit the like button and subscribe — I release a new golf lesson every single week to help you strike the ball better and play more consistent golf.
📩 Want personalised help with your fairway woods? Head to https://www.dannymaude.com for lessons and coaching.

videos.title: Reset Your Body in 14 Days (No Sugar)
videos.summary_source_text:
Reset Your Body in 14 Days (No Sugar) What happens when you stop eating sugar for 14 days? Most people quit sugar right before the benefits begin. Discover how a sugar detox affects your body and can transform your health.

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/4uCDM90

0:00 Introduction: What happens when you stop eating sugar? 
0:20 Why you should stop eating sugar
0:40 Insulin resistance
1:00 Sugar detox day 1
5:20 The benefits of quitting sugar day 5 
7:42 How to continue healthy eating after cutting out sugar 

Keep watching to learn how to break sugar cravings, avoid hidden sugars, and support healthy weight loss naturally:
▶️ https://youtu.be/iG67l-wqOV8
▶️ https://youtu.be/LoKAhLfPUYA
▶️ https://youtu.be/G1Z4703YstY

Learn how to read and understand food labels with this free guide: https://drbrg.co/4wk8ENg

Keto Recipes: https://www.youtube.com/@drbergketorecipes 

Most people who try to quit sugar fail around day 3 or 4. This is often when the brain’s reward system is at its lowest point, causing many people to give up right before things start to improve.

Sugar can combine with proteins in the body through a process called glycation, permanently damaging tissues and nerve endings. Over time, a high-sugar diet can also contribute to insulin resistance.

When quitting sugar, it’s important to eliminate hidden sugars as well. Starches are a common hidden sugar found in many ultra-processed foods.

Brain fog, irritability, and fatigue are common during a sugar detox and often drive people back to sugar. This happens because the brain is craving dopamine and pushing you to consume more sugar.

Around day 5, many people begin experiencing the benefits of quitting sugar, including reduced cravings, lower inflammation, improved mental clarity, better energy, and weight loss.

B vitamins, potassium, and magnesium may also help reduce sugar cravings and support the body during the transition.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

videos.title: Which golf ball should YOU buy 🤔
videos.summary_source_text:
Which golf ball should YOU buy 🤔 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:

videos.title: How to Lower Blood Pressure Naturally Just by Breathing (and a Tool that Train You)
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Lower Blood Pressure Naturally Just by Breathing (and a Tool that Train You) Today I want to share a simple, natural way to lower your blood pressure by tapping into the power of your vagus nerve. With nearly half of the population dealing with hypertension, I see too many people struggling to address the underlying cause of their condition. In this video, I'll walk you through how a specific breathing technique and a device I recommend to my own patients can bring your blood pressure down naturally in just 15 minutes a day.

The device I recommend is called Resp-e-Rate. You can learn more about it and receive a special discount by using this link:
https://resperate.com/hcp?aff=drpradip

Please note that Dr. Jamnadas participates in an affiliate program with the Res-e-Rate device. This means that if you choose to purchase through the provided link or use the discount code, he may receive a commission at no additional cost to you. The discount is provided as a benefit to viewers.

All opinions expressed are based on clinical experience and are not influenced by affiliate partnerships.

This video is for informational and educational purposes only and is not intended as medical advice. Always consult with your physician or qualified healthcare provider before making any changes to your health regimen.

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.

I enjoy providing educational videos to all of you, but I want to ensure that you are aware that only the official accounts authorized to promote and share my content are Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, Aristotle Education, LLC, The Galen Foundation, and Cardiovascular Interventions. On YouTube there are only two official channels:

@pradipjamnadasmd and @thegalenfoundation

There are numerous accounts, videos, and books circulating that use my image or claim to represent my work. In this age of AI, I encourage you to stay vigilant and take a moment to verify the source before believing or sharing any information not posted or confirmed through my official channels. Please protect yourself from consuming false and untrustworthy medical information.

All of my official links can be found here: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

14:22 Why This Approach Works

videos.title: The Warning Signs in Your Feet You Should NEVER Ignore
videos.summary_source_text:
The Warning Signs in Your Feet You Should NEVER Ignore Did you know you can spot liver problems in your feet?

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

👉 Get Dr. Berg’s Daily Routine as a free download: https://drbrg.co/4sR9AWf

Timestamps: 
0:00 Liver problems can show up in your feet; here’s what to look for
2:40 Itching of the bottom of your foot indicates a backup of fluid into the liver
4:44 Here are some causes of liver problems
5:40 I recommend a good diet for your liver

Because liver problems often show up in your feet, I explain the signs and symptoms to look for. For a full diagnosis of liver disease, please see your doctor.

Red and brown dots, which can also be shiny, can appear on the lower leg. This indicates poor circulation. It could also be diabetes, but you see this a lot in people with liver problems.

You may also see spider veins (spider nevus). This can be an indication of too much estrogen in the liver. If your heels are cracked, this is usually a vitamin B3 deficiency or an omega 3 fatty acid deficiency. One function of the liver is to make bile to help your body absorb fatty acids including vitamin A and vitamin E.

Itching on the bottom of your foot indicates a backup of fluids into the liver. For example, if bile backs up and your liver is congested, you could be getting a build up of histamines. 

Pain or inflammation in the bottom of the foot often mimics plantar fasciitis, but can be a liver problem. You can also have hot feet, and bad odor when your liver can’t detoxify correctly so toxins are leaking out through your skin. 

Usually if someone’s liver is bad, it means their diet is bad, and their body is full of toxins. Chances are, if your liver is bad so too are your kidneys and colon. 

If you have pitting edema is also a common liver symptom. You may also have toenail fungus. People with liver problems often have more fungus in and on their body, due to an imbalance of their gut microflora. You may also experience dandruff and psoriasis. Your nail beds turn white.

Common causes of liver problems include medications, sugar (especially high fructose corn syrup), cooked foods, too few vegetables, excessive alcohol, and excessive protein. 

A healthy ketogenic diet combined with intermittent fasting is best for a healthy liver.

Now you know how to spot liver problems, understand their causes, and how to get your liver healthy again.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

videos.title: How did BMW get it so WRONG? M3 v M5 DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
How did BMW get it so WRONG? M3 v M5 DRAG RACE Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-0905

It’s a BMW estate head-to-head! 

We’ve got our hands on the BMW M3 CS Touring, and it’s going up against Mat’s long-term BMW M5 Touring test car! 

The question is, how different are these two long-bodied BMWs? Let’s check out the stats… 

The BMW M5 Touring houses a 4.4-litre twin-turbo V8, coupled with an electric motor. The two combine to produce 727hp and 1,000Nm, and this power is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic gearbox. All in it costs from £116,160, and it’s incredibly heavy, tipping the scales at 2,475kg! 

Then we come to the M3 CS Touring. Under the bonnet, it ‘only’ has a 3-litre twin-turbo straight-six, and this creates 550hp and 650Nm. Power is once again sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic gearbox, and it costs almost the same as the M5, costing £120,600. However, it’s a whole lot lighter than the M5, weighing in at 1,850kg. 

So the M5 has more power, and an electric motor - but it weighs almost 650kg more than the M3! Can you pick a winner? There’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE!

Ultimate BWM M drag race: https://bit.ly/BMW-M-Drag-Race-0905 Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-0905

videos.title: När norske Tordenskjold anföll Göteborg
videos.summary_source_text:
När norske Tordenskjold anföll Göteborg 00:00-00:40   Intro
00:40-01:15   Karl XII:s död
01:15-04:37   Kaparkriget och Lasse i Gatan
04:38-05:27   Peder Tordenskjold
05:28-06:11   Anfallet mot Nya Varvet 1717
06:12-10:00   Anfallet mot Nya Älvsborg och Marstrand 1719
10:01-13:55   Tordenskjolds sista sjöstrid

NÄR TORDENSKJOLD ANFÖLL GÖTEBORG
Sjökrig, kapare och stormaktstid

Kung Karl XII:s död vid Fredriksten i Norge 1718 innebar slutet för den svenska stormaktstiden. Men kriget på västkusten fortsatte, inte minst på grund av den livliga kaperiverksamheten, som hade sitt centrum i Göteborg. Lars Gathenhielm - Lasse i Gatan - var den mest kände kaparen och hans namn vilar tungt över Majorna.

De svenska kaparna blev ett svårt gissel för den dansk-norska handelssjöfarten och under ledning av Peder Tordenskjold, ursprungligen från Trondheim, anfölls Nya Varvet, Marstrand och Nya Älvsborgs fästning. Tordenskjolds sista sjöstrid utspelade sig i Göteborg och i tornet på Nya Älvsborg sitter fortfarande kanonkulor från belägringen 1719.

videos.title: UK | 08th May Friday - RIFT Before You Quit – Ep4 | Understanding Alpha Flow, Smart Flow & Microflow
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 08th May Friday - RIFT Before You Quit – Ep4 | Understanding Alpha Flow, Smart Flow & Microflow In Episode 4 of the “RIFT Before You Quit” series, we take a deep dive into the core execution architecture behind the RIFT automated trading engine and explain why professional trading systems rely on diversified trading behavior instead of a single strategy model.

This session breaks down:

Why single-strategy trading systems eventually fail
The difference between retail trading behavior and institutional execution models
How RIFT’s Alpha Flow, Smart Flow, and Microflow are designed to adapt to changing market conditions
Why market volatility, liquidity, and exposure management matter
How professional systems diversify execution behavior rather than simply diversifying assets

Topics covered include:
✅ Alpha Flow – High momentum participation during volatility expansion
✅ Smart Flow – Balanced execution for consistency in rotational markets
✅ Microflow – Precision exposure management during uncertainty and unstable conditions
✅ Institutional vs Retail trading behavior
✅ Dynamic position sizing and fragmented execution models
✅ Diversified strategic portfolio allocation examples
✅ Why adaptive execution architecture is the future of automated trading

The livestream also explores real market examples using:

Tesla volatility conditions
S&P 500 rotational environments
Federal Reserve / FOMC uncertainty events
NASDAQ heat maps
VIX Index behavior

This educational session is designed to help users, leaders, and affiliate marketers better understand the technology and strategic structure behind the RIFT ecosystem so they can present the platform professionally and confidently. In Episode 4 of the “RIFT Before You Quit” series, we take a deep dive into the core execution architecture behind the RIFT automated trading engine and explain why professional trading systems rely on diversified trading behavior instead of a single strategy model.

This session breaks down:

Why single-strategy trading systems eventually fail
The difference between retail trading behavior and institutional execution models
How RIFT’s Alpha Flow, Smart Flow, and Microflow are designed to adapt to changing market conditions
Why market volatility, liquidity, and exposure management matter
How professional systems diversify execution behavior rather than simply diversifying assets

Topics covered include:
✅ Alpha Flow – High momentum participation during volatility expansion
✅ Smart Flow – Balanced execution for consistency in rotational markets
✅ Microflow – Precision exposure management during uncertainty and unstable conditions
✅ Institutional vs Retail trading behavior
✅ Dynamic position sizing and fragmented execution models
✅ Diversified strategic portfolio allocation examples
✅ Why adaptive execution architecture is the future of automated trading

The livestream also explores real market examples using:

Tesla volatility conditions
S&P 500 rotational environments
Federal Reserve / FOMC uncertainty events
NASDAQ heat maps
VIX Index behavior

This educational session is designed to help users, leaders, and affiliate marketers better understand the technology and strategic structure behind the RIFT ecosystem so they can present the platform professionally and confidently.

videos.title: 8.5: Dobes stops 28 of 29 shots to even series at 1! 🇨🇿⭐
videos.summary_source_text:
8.5: Dobes stops 28 of 29 shots to even series at 1! 🇨🇿⭐ Another game, another Jakub Dobes show-stealing performance! This time, the Czech phenom turns away 28 of 29 shots the Sabres send his way to lead his Canadiens to a critical, series-tying 5-1 victory. With the win, Dobes becomes just the third Montreal rookie goaltender over the past 40 years to tally 5 or more wins in a single postseason, joining Patrick Roy (15 in 1986) and Carey Price (5 in 2008). Dobes' heroics between the irons earn him both 2nd star of the game and Top European Player of the Night honors!

videos.title: Hindi | 08th May, Friday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 08th May, Friday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: OpenAI Just Dropped The Biggest Voice AI Upgrade Yet
videos.summary_source_text:
OpenAI Just Dropped The Biggest Voice AI Upgrade Yet 👉 Try CodeRabbit here: https://coderabbit.link/ai-revolution
OpenAI just launched new real-time voice AI that can talk, translate, transcribe, and take action while people are speaking. At the same time, OpenAI revealed MRC, the hidden supercomputer networking tech helping train frontier models, while Sam Altman admitted some companies may be blaming layoffs on AI even when the real story is more complicated.

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@science.revolution

🧠 What You’ll See
0:00 Intro
00:24 How OpenAI’s new real-time voice models can talk, translate, transcribe, and take action
SOURCE: https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/
1:04 Why GPT-Realtime-2 is being built for live voice agents and real-world tasks
SOURCE: https://developers.openai.com/cookbook/examples/voice_solutions/realtime_agents_guide
7:56 How OpenAI’s MRC networking protocol keeps massive AI supercomputers running
SOURCE: https://openai.com/index/mrc-supercomputer-networking/
12:42 Why Sam Altman says some companies are using AI as a layoff excuse
SOURCE: https://www.techradar.com/pro/sam-altman-says-some-companies-are-ai-washing-by-blaming-unrelated-layoffs-on-the-technology-but-admits-things-may-get-worse-soon
Why the AI jobs debate is still messy and far from settled
SOURCE: https://budgetlab.yale.edu/research/tracking-impact-ai-labor-market

🚨 Why It Matters
OpenAI’s new voice models show where AI assistants are heading next: real conversations, live translation, instant transcription, and tool-based actions in the same workflow. MRC shows the huge infrastructure race behind those models, where GPU networks now matter almost as much as the chips themselves. And the jobs debate shows the uncomfortable part: AI may already be changing some work, while some companies may also be using it as a convenient excuse.

videos.title: Struggling With Your Soft Drag?  Fix It Fast!
videos.summary_source_text:
Struggling With Your Soft Drag?  Fix It Fast! The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: From Worst Player on Her B Team to Making AAA in 2.5 Years
videos.summary_source_text:
From Worst Player on Her B Team to Making AAA in 2.5 Years From Worst Player on Her B Team to Making AAA in 2.5 Years

Ben Frank has coached hockey for over 20 years. He played at the University of Toronto, has a kinesiology degree, has run double-A and triple-A teams, and is currently the president of the Junior Reign Youth Hockey Club in Southern California.

He thought he had all the answers when his daughter Riley started playing hockey at 10. Two years later, she was still the weakest player on her B team. Then Ben found Train 2.0 — and within 3 years, Riley went from the worst player on her B team to making the U16A Lady Ducks (the defending state champion team), getting selected for the U15 Multi-District High Performance Camp in Las Vegas, and being evaluated by USA Hockey's national development staff.

In this episode of the Train 2.0 Show, Kevin McKinnon sits down with Ben to break down:
→ Why a 20-year coach with a kinesiology degree was missing key mechanics.
→ The skepticism Ben had when he first found Train 2.0 (and how he tested it secretly).
→ The mechanics that changed everything: the soft drag, the anchor, the shuffle.
→ Why coaches kept saying "Riley always has her head up" after the mechanics clicked.
→ How hockey sense improved as a side effect of better movement.
→ Why parents started asking "What's happening with Riley?" 
→ Advice for coaches on the fence about Train 2.0 certification.

If you're a hockey dad, a hockey coach, or a parent watching your kid stuck at one level, this episode is for you.

videos.title: The $250 Million ChatGPT Prompt: How One CEO Asked AI to Help Him Commit Fraud
videos.summary_source_text:
The $250 Million ChatGPT Prompt: How One CEO Asked AI to Help Him Commit Fraud A South Korean gaming CEO asked ChatGPT how to escape a $250 million contract.

He followed its advice.

A Delaware judge unwound the entire scheme — and the chat logs he thought he deleted became evidence in court.

This is Part 2 of the series on what happens when people treat generative AI like a confidential advisor instead of what it actually is: a stenographer with no loyalty, building a forensically recoverable record of every decision you workshop with it.

Every prompt you type is a timestamped, recoverable, court-admissible transcript of your decision-making.

You're not consulting an advisor. You're training a witness for the prosecution.


▶ Watch Complete Video: https://youtu.be/n6R7005Y-z8

🔔 Subscribe to The Late Byte with Robi: https://www.youtube.com/@TheLateBytewithRobi?sub_confirmation=1

videos.title: Grok 5 Explained: Is Musk's 10% AGI Claim Actually Real?
videos.summary_source_text:
Grok 5 Explained: Is Musk's 10% AGI Claim Actually Real? Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Elon Musk says Grok 5 has a 10% chance of becoming AGI — but is that number real, or just hype? In this deep dive, I break down exactly what Musk claimed, what Grok 5 actually is under the hood, and whether the 10% AGI probability holds up under scrutiny.

We cover the leaked specs (6 trillion parameters, 10T variant in the pipeline), Colossus 2 — xAI's million-GPU supercomputer, multimodal training with real-time video understanding, ARC-AGI benchmark performance, and what top researchers like Andrej Karpathy, Yann LeCun, and Yoshua Bengio are actually saying about Musk's prediction.

If you're trying to figure out whether Grok 5 is the AGI moment or just the next Musk timeline that slips by 18 months, this video gives you the full picture — specs, timeline, expert pushback, and what it means for your job and industry.

videos.title: 1 cup oatmeal and oranges / No white sugar or white flour! I haven't eaten sugar for years!
videos.summary_source_text:
1 cup oatmeal and oranges / No white sugar or white flour! I haven't eaten sugar for years! 1 cup oatmeal and oranges / No white sugar or white flour! I haven't eaten sugar for years!

2 oranges.
1 cup (120 g) rolled oats.
1 tsp baking powder.
1/4 cup (30 g) shredded coconut.
Salt.
Olive oil.
Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (180°C) for about 40 minutes.
3.5 oz (100 g) sugar-free dark chocolate.
1/3 cup (70 g) almonds.

videos.title: Round 2 Highlights | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship
videos.summary_source_text:
Round 2 Highlights | 2026 Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship Highlights from round two of the Estrella Damm Catalunya Championship, where a host of Spanish stars battle to make the cut in Barcelona including local hero Pablo Larrazábal as the nine-time DP World Tour winner tees it up at his home club.

► SUBSCRIBE: http://et.golf/Subscribe 

The DP World Tour is back to where it all started this week. Spain was the host of the first ever DP World Tour - then European Tour - event in 1972 so it is fitting that it is where the 2026 European Swing gets started. The fourth of five Global Swings on the 2026 Race to Dubai, the European Swing consists of six counting events - four of which are national opens - with the US PGA Championship and U.S. Open also happening within the same timeframe. Spain has long been one of the world's great golfing nations and this week local heroes will take centre stage, with a string of established and rising stars searching for home glory. Nacho Elvira has already won in 2026 and will be hungry for more, as will fellow winners in recent seasons Eugenio Chacarra, Alejandro del Rey, Angel Hidalgo and Pablo Larrazábal.

Welcome to the Official YouTube channel of the DP World Tour. Every week you can find extended tournament highlights and individual player highlights from the likes of Rory McIlroy, Justin Rose and Tommy Fleetwood. This is also the home of the 14 Club Challenge, Little Interviews, Fastest Hole and many more challenges, pranks and world record attempts featuring the biggest golfers in the world.

► FACEBOOK: https://www.facebook.com/DPWorldTour

videos.title: You Can't Steal a Charity — Musk vs OpenAI Trial Explained
videos.summary_source_text:
You Can't Steal a Charity — Musk vs OpenAI Trial Explained Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Everyone thinks the Musk vs. Altman trial is just another billionaire feud. It's not. The verdict in this Oakland courtroom could quietly reshape how every AI company on the planet gets built, funded, and governed for the next decade — and almost nobody is connecting the dots.

In this deep dive, I break down what's actually happening inside the trial: the four legal landmines no one is covering, the bombshell moments from the courtroom (including Greg Brockman's "warm to steal the nonprofit" journal entry), and the three verdict scenarios that could land — each one sending shockwaves through the AI industry in completely different ways.

By the end, you'll understand this trial better than 99% of the headlines you'll read tomorrow.

⏱️ TIMESTAMPS
00:00 - Why This Trial Matters More Than You Think

04:39 - Inside the Courtroom — What Actually Happened

08:08 - Why This Actually Matters to You

✔ The 2015 founding agreement Musk says was betrayed

The Dr. Berg LIVE Show - May 8, 2026 When it comes to your health, most people try to fix everything at once. But there are 10 biological signals that control how you feel every day, and only 1 of them matters most to your body.

Take my free 2-minute quiz and discover which signal YOU need to fix first: https://www.drberg.com/health-lever-quiz?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=may_26_live_show&utm_content=description

videos.summary_source_text:
🗣️ ”Mest belånad vinner” Utan sammanhang låter det väldigt riskabelt men Ricard Nylund, finansiell rådgivare ser det utifrån perspektivet att belåning är en viktig del i en lyckad ekonomi. Många av oss äger bostäder som med sin hävstång (bolån) skapar en väldigt fin grund.

Vi brukar också tjata om att det finns en risk med att amortera för mycket då du kan sitta i en villa för X miljoner men ha väldigt lite ekonomisk rörlighet då alla din pengar finns låsta i bostaden och att belåna en bostad på äldre dagar är ofta svårt/omöjligt. videos.title: Kvinnor får sämre rådgivning än män.
videos.summary_source_text:
Kvinnor får sämre rådgivning än män. Två personer. Samma ekonomi.
Olika råd – bara på grund av kön.

En studie på 27 000 bankmöten visar att kvinnor:
– får färre rabatter
– rekommenderas dyrare fonder
– oftare styrs mot bankens egna produkter

Och ja – det kostar.

≈ 38 000 – 40 000 kror per 100 000 investerade kronor över 20 år
… bara i avgifter.

Det mest obehagliga?
Skillnaden ligger inte i behov – utan i hur kunden uppfattas.

Det här kallas statistisk diskriminering:
rådgivaren gissar din kunskap istället för att ta reda på den.

Det är inte alltid illvilja.
Men det blir ändå dyrt.

👉 Vad kan du göra?

– Jämför med en billig indexfond
– Träffa fler än en rådgivare

Eller enklare, spara:
→ brett
→ billigt
→ långsiktigt

i en passiv global indexfond eller fondrobot.

Tack till dig som supporter – du gör att vi kan bryta ner sånt här.

👉 Har du varit med om något liknande?

Källa:
Bucher-Koenen, T., Hackethal, A., Koenen, J. och Laudenbach, C. (2025) ‘Gender Differences in Financial Advice’, American Economic Review, 115(12), s. 4218–4252. doi: 10.1257/aer.20211024.

#privatekonomi #sparande #investera #jämställdhet #indexfonder #pengar #rikaTillsammans #spargap Två personer. Samma ekonomi.

videos.title: Apple Pulled This... I Run AI On It
videos.summary_source_text:
Apple Pulled This... I Run AI On It 🔗 Get the full build, every command, every config inside the Creator Magic community → https://mrc.fm/closetai 🧠⚡

I went fully off-grid with AI in this one. Frontier models like Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 are incredible, but every call my OpenClaw and Hermes agents make is hitting someone else's server and paying someone else's bill — so I asked the dangerous question: can I get near-frontier intelligence running entirely on a machine in my own house that nobody can throttle, rate limit, or take away? Right before Apple quietly pulled the 256GB Mac mini M4 and the 256GB RAM Mac Studio M3 Ultra from their website, I managed to grab the exact config I needed, and in this video I set it up from scratch step by step — power settings, headless SSH, static hostname, Homebrew, Ollama, LM Studio, MLX — so you can copy exactly what I did. Then I throw real agent prompts at Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, GPT-OSS-120B, Gemma 4 and MiniMax M2.7 to find out which ones actually replace frontier work, before plugging the whole thing directly into my OpenClaw and Hermes stack so my agents are now running 100% local — silent, sub-100W, in a closet, forever. They tried to take away my cloud intelligence. They will never take away the intelligence I own.

Golf tips for seniors who want effortless power and more distance start with one make-or-break move — and Eric Cogorno breaks it down into 3 simple steps any golfer over 50 can do.

In This Video, You'll Discover:
• Why the senior golf swing lives or dies in the first half of the backswing — and the one core move that fixes it immediately
• How to use the club-in-the-stomach golf drill (learned from instructor JT Thomas) to build an easy golf swing for seniors that generates power before the downswing even begins
• The exact trail foot position — toes flared, pulled back to the midpoint of your lead foot — that unlocks effortless power and reduces strain
• How Sam Snead's setup and backswing model helped him play elite golf well into his 60s, and how you can use the same blueprint today
• The right shoulder retraction move that dramatically increases your turn and creates a bigger backswing — no extra flexibility required
• Golf drills you can practice anywhere to groove a repeatable, powerful backswing sequence from setup through the top
• A complete 3-step system from golf lessons and live golf school experience with over 10,000 students — proven to add distance without added effort

Eric Cogorno has built this 3-part framework from years of hands-on golf school and online coaching experience, watching the same backswing breakdowns cost senior golfers distance they don't have to lose. The core turn, trail foot position, and right shoulder depth covered in this video address the root cause — not the symptoms — of a weak, effortful golf swing. Whether you're trying to get back yards you've lost or simply make the game feel easier, these are the foundational pieces. For more on building a powerful, efficient swing, check out Eric's related driver distance videos on the channel.

videos.title: The HIDDEN Cause of Chronic Phlegmy (Mucus) Throat
videos.summary_source_text:
The HIDDEN Cause of Chronic Phlegmy (Mucus) Throat If you’re dealing with chronic phlegm or mucus in the throat, this is for you. Find out how to get rid of phlegm and excess mucus to finally put a stop to constant throat clearing. 

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/499EzWT

0:00 Introduction: Chronic phlegm and mucus buildup  
0:18 Post-nasal drip and sinus drainage 
0:30 Silent acid reflux mucus
1:34 The hidden cause of phlegm 
3:37 The vagus nerve and chronic mucus 
4:01 Other causes of excess mucus  
4:53 Determining the cause of chronic mucus
5:38 Natural remedies for phlegm 
6:00 Betaine hydrochloride 

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements

Watch more videos on mucus, digestion, and the real cause of heartburn:
▶️ https://youtu.be/t8y4-wR0ajo
▶️ https://youtu.be/MzPw7UvAcio
▶️ https://youtu.be/xcBoVYJh_QA

Constantly clearing your throat? If decongestants and antihistamines aren’t working, the real problem may not be post-nasal drip or your lungs; it could be silent reflux.

Many cases of chronic phlegm and throat clearing are caused by silent reflux, where digestive enzymes like pepsin irritate the throat and vocal cords. In response, the body produces inflammatory mucus as a protective mechanism.

Silent reflux can occur when the valve at the top of the stomach isn’t functioning properly. This may be related to SIBO, but more commonly it’s linked to low stomach acid. When stomach acid isn’t acidic enough, and the pH becomes too high, the valve can lose its ability to stay closed properly.

Antacids may provide temporary relief, but they can further weaken stomach acid and worsen the underlying problem over time.

The stomach valve and digestive processes are also heavily influenced by the vagus nerve, which controls the parasympathetic “rest-and-digest” system. 

Chronic stress can interfere with this system and contribute to silent reflux symptoms.
Frequent eating can further weaken stomach acid, and the problem often becomes more common with age.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

The EARLIEST Sign of Dementia (Not What You Think) Most people think dementia starts with memory loss… but that’s not true.

By the time you begin forgetting names, misplacing things, or feeling confused, the disease may have already been developing in your brain for 10 to 20 years.

👉 Get Dr. Berg’s Daily Routine as a free download: https://drbrg.co/4v67Kn4

Researchers have found that dementia doesn’t begin in the memory centers of the brain: it starts elsewhere, affecting other functions long before memory decline appears.

2. Lägg två limehalvor på stekbordet med snittsidan nedåt och låt dem bli riktigt bruna. Pressa dem sen över de färdiga räkorna för mer karamellig smak.

3. Låt räkorna marinera i olja, vitlök och chili i kylen hela natten för smakfestival!
